description_long: This is a bulbous, wide-bodied ceramic vase with a narrow neck and a flared, uneven rim. The surface is heavily weathered, showing a patchwork of lighter and darker brown tones, likely from age and restoration. The body is asymmetrical, with visible cracks and repairs, suggesting it is an ancient or archaeological piece. The texture appears rough and matte, with no visible glaze or decorative embellishments. There are no handles or additional features, and the opening is wide enough for use as a receptacle for flowers or other items. The overall form is utilitarian, with a rustic, historical character.
description: A weathered, bulbous ceramic vase with a narrow neck, flared rim, and patchy brown surface showing visible cracks and repairs.
description_view_0: This view highlights the uneven rim and prominent cracks on the upper left side, with a visible patchwork of restoration.
description_view_1: The front shows the widest part of the body and the most pronounced surface wear, with the rim appearing slightly more open.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the asymmetry of the vase and the extensive surface cracking on the right side.
description_view_3: The back view displays a smoother surface with fewer visible cracks, and the bulbous shape is most apparent.
